3.why: We need the index of the last line.

4.why: No container has been measured yet.
4.invariant: mx is the largest area found so far.

5.happening: Place the left wall lp at index 0.
5.why: Begin with the widest possible container.

6.happening: Place the right wall rp at index {rp}.
6.why: The two walls start as far apart as possible.

7.why: There is still width between the walls to try.
Width is 0; no wider container remains.
7.invariant: Every useful pair has been tried.

8.why: Water spills over the shorter of the two walls.
8.invariant: h is capped by the shorter side.

9.why: The horizontal distance between the two walls.

10.why: This is how much water this container holds.
10.invariant: Comparing against the best so far ({mx}).

11.why: This container holds more water than any seen before.

11.happening: {area} does not beat {mx} — keep the current best.
11.why: A previous container was at least as good.

12.happening: Left wall ({height[lp]}) is shorter than right ({height[rp]}).
12.why: Only moving the shorter wall can ever increase the area.

12.happening: Right wall ({height[rp]}) is no taller than left ({height[lp]}).
12.why: Move the shorter (right) wall, hoping for a taller one.

13.why: Discard the shorter left wall and try the next line.

15.why: Discard the shorter right wall and try the next line.

16.why: Every candidate width has been checked.

loop.why: Test the next pair of walls.

move.why: Only that can increase the area.
